document_info.title: Session Programme: Passing and Receiving to Score
document_info.topic: Attacking Combinations, Creative Receiving, and Finishing

session_practices.name: Technical Warm-Up: Corner Passing & Receiving Rotations

pitch_diagram.image_description: A square 20x20m training area with cones marking the four corners and a small central diamond of markers. Players are distributed across the corner stations, circulating two balls simultaneously in a clockwise pattern through directional combinations.

tactical_layout.central_element: A 3x3m central diamond gate used as an awareness reference
tactical_layout.stations: 4 corner stations labeled clockwise A, B, C, and D

setup.player_distribution: Players line up evenly behind the four corner cones. Two balls start at opposite corners (A and C) to maintain high technical density and constant focus.

session_practices.explanation: Player A passes diagonally to Player B, who checked off their cone. Player B receives on their back foot, opens their body shape, and fires a pass to Player C. Player C lays it off into the path of Player D making an overlapping run. Players follow their pass to rotate to the next station.
coaching_points: Receive the ball on the back foot with an open body shape to look forward immediately.
Passes must be delivered with proper weight to the safe side of the teammate, away from an imaginary defender.
Check away from the cone before receiving to simulate escaping a tight marker.

session_practices.name: Directional 4v2 Grid Transition Game

pitch_diagram.image_description: A 30x15m grid divided into two equal 15x15m squares. 4 attacking players (blue) look to keep possession against 2 active defenders (orange) inside one half, trying to complete a sequence before transferring the ball across the dividing line to target teammates.

setup.player_roles: 4 attackers look to create maximum width and depth in the active zone vs. 2 trailing defenders, while 2 resting defenders wait in the opposite half.

session_practices.explanation: The 4 blue attackers look to complete 4 consecutive passes inside their box against 2 orange defenders. Once achieved, they look for a opening to pass across the line to their forward target in the other box. Two attackers immediately sprint across to support their teammate, creating a new 4v2 in the opposite zone, while the other 2 defenders step up to press.
coaching_points: Create passing angles immediately by adjusting your support position relative to the ball and the defenders.
Recognize when to play safe to retain possession vs. when to execute a penetrative pass into the next zone.
Defenders must shift quickly to seal the central line and block forward passing channels.

session_practices.name: Phase of Play: 3v2 to 4v3 Crossing & Finishing

pitch_diagram.pitch_type: Half Pitch with Dual Flank Windows
pitch_diagram.image_description: An attacking phase of play taking place inside the final third of a standard field. A full goal with a keeper is at the base. Central lines outline a tight box for a 3v2 central overload, while 2 wide flank channels are designated for overlapping wingers.

setup.player_distribution: 3 central attackers and 1 wide winger face 2 central defenders. 1 defensive midfielder tracks back from the edge of the final third zone to alter defensive balance.
setup.starting_point: Play triggers from a central deep playmaker serving a pass into the active attacking box.

session_practices.explanation: The 3 central attackers combine to draw the 2 defenders out of position. They can choose to attack the goal directly via quick combinations, or split the ball wide to the winger. If the ball moves wide, a fullback overlaps to deliver a cross. The central attackers must cross their runs to attack the front post, back post, and penalty spot.
coaching_points: Attacking runs into the box must be dynamic, explosive, and synchronized with the wide cross delivery.
The winger must assess the defensive block line before choosing between a driven low cross, a chipped ball to the back post, or a cut-back.
Attackers should look for rebounds and second-ball scraps if the initial shot is blocked or parried by the goalkeeper.

session_practices.name: 11v11 Game with Attacking Overload Scoring Conditions

pitch_diagram.image_description: A full-field 11v11 tactical match. The pitch features a dashed line across the final 25 meters on both ends to designate a specific attacking zone where special scoring rules apply to reward creative forward movement.

setup.initial_alignment: Both squads line up in their standard match formations (e.g., 4-3-3 vs. 4-4-2), playing with full competitive rules.

session_practices.explanation: A regular game with one key tactical constraint: any goal scored from a third-man combination or a creative back-foot turn that penetrates the 25-meter attacking line counts for 3 goals. This constraint encourages players to look for progressive passes into pockets of space and rewards forward risks.
coaching_points: Midfielders must constantly scan the pitch to spot vertical passing lanes into the forwards early.
Forwards must play on different lines, with one dropping short to drag a center-back out while the other breaks into the space behind.
Maintain defensive shape and structure behind the ball to control the defensive transition if possession is lost.
